title: Bitter Chewy Russian Veal Dumplings
description: Mildly bitter, chewy dumplings with a kid-friendly flavor profile, featuring veal, fenugreek, and a twist of dill for authentic Russian taste.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, salt, pepper, fenugreek seeds, and water to form a dough. Knead until smooth, cover, and let rest 10 minutes.
In another bowl, combine ground veal, grated carrot, chopped onion, fresh dill, and egg. Mix thoroughly until homogeneous.
Divide dough into 12 equal pieces. Roll each piece on a lightly floured surface into 3mm thick rounds about 6cm in diameter.
Place a heaping teaspoon of the veal mixture onto the center of each dough round. Fold dough over to form a half-moon, pressing edges to seal and twist to secure.
Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add dumplings in batches, browning 2-3 minutes per side.
After browning, pour 2 tablespoons water into the skillet, cover, and let dumplings steam for 5 minutes, ensuring the veal reaches 160°F internal temperature.
Remove the lid, allow any excess liquid to evaporate, and continue cooking for 1 minute to crisp edges.
Plate dumplings and optionally drizzle with a mild sauce such as light yogurt or a sweet tomato-pickle blend.
Serve immediately hot; leftovers can be reheated gently in a skillet or microwave.
For a plant-based variant, replace veal with an equal weight of crumbled tofu, and follow the same cooking procedure.
